title: The sad downslide was so unexpected
display_title: The sad downslide was so unexpected
wikitext:
[]: The sad downslide was so unexpected             Quote Post by MisatoCrush » Fri Jan 24, 2014 8:26 am My first exposure to anime was Gundam Wing Mobile Suit. Fairly happy and whatever. When I first saw the NGE tv show and EoE I had no clue that a horrifically sad down-spin was about to happen. I mean, there were such cute goofy moments in the TV show. If NGE had been all happy go lucky and followed a traditional Mecha story arc I wouldn't hold it nearly as high as I do. It was only a week or so in the past that I finished watching it all for the first time. Upon further reflection I'm extremely surprised that I didn't cry during the show. Pretty sure I will the second time through. I plan on drinking a bottle of red wine while diving into EoE for the 2nd time as well. I love NGE even more because it made me feel something damnit! It made me feel alive and made me feel emotions very strongly. There is a famous South Korean movie director named Chan-wook Park. I read an interview with him where he was asked, 'Why do you make movies with such tragic or grotesque stories?'. He answered that when he pays money to go see a movie, he doesn't want to sit there and go, 'Oh, how cute. Oh isn't that nice.' He wants to FEEL SOMETHING. So those are the kinds of films that he makes. Films that will cause a very strong reaction and emotion in the viewer. I think the same can be said for NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by amitakartok » Fri Jan 24, 2014 10:59 am Yup. I think Anno understands that as well.

[]:             Quote Post by FriedlCore » Fri Jan 24, 2014 12:07 pm You sum up pretty well why this is such a lovable series. I was pretty affected, too. It touched me even more than Requiem for a Dream, a Movie after which I haven't slept for two days

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Jan 24, 2014 1:00 pm You're definitely right about the feelings part. It's part of why I can't stand romance comedy movies. I like Romance, I lioke comedy, but all alone with little else, and generally little variation within the Genre, they just don't do it again. Give me some horror fantasy plot for the two to fight in between arguing and smooching, or something. Interestingly, Anno initially planned for an "earn your happy ending" scenario (In a way, it still is, but you won't see that right away and it's sure very far from "the characters are shown happily married or walking into the sunset") but then it seems he got carried away, or reached the conclusion that the characters he had created would lead to another logical conclusion and didn't want to force a less realistic one, who knows... One interview (not with Anno tho. I think it was Tsurumaki) explicitly mentions them not wanting to "lie". The power and relative uniqueness of EVA lies in the parabole (or S curve?): Things get worse after they got better. If Shinji had stayed alone moping in his room, if Rei has stayed going through an unvalued existence the whole time, i think we'd be less affected. It's that we saw promise, that there was potential that is painfully lost, that makes EVA so effective.

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Fri Jan 24, 2014 2:24 pm Kendrix wrote: Interestingly, Anno initially planned for an "earn your happy ending" scenario

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Fri Jan 24, 2014 4:09 pm Amagad, 4 different people from germany in a row!

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Fri Jan 24, 2014 4:19 pm qu4d wrote: Amagad, 4 different people from germany in a row!

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Fri Jan 24, 2014 4:32 pm Dataprime wrote:

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Thu Jan 30, 2014 4:30 am Not to be a contrarian but if NGE ended as a complete downer a la Angel's Egg i think i would admire it much less than i do. Generally speaking, the main reason i like anime is because it is not afraid of bringing forth an honestly positive or kind-spirited tone in it. I have a very hard time imagining something like K-On! outside of anime.

If Shinji had stayed alone moping in his room, if Rei has stayed going through an unvalued existence the whole time, i think we'd be less affected.
